mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to equity cash flows, mechanically compressing valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the unemployment rate signals an approaching labor market peak and impending earnings recession, causing equity valuations to contract as investors price in higher recession probabilities over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the rising unemployment signal, or the signal shows no predictive power for earnings revisions.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between housing starts and consumer credit growth indicates a structural shift in aggregate demand composition. When housing accelerates while credit stagnates, corporate profit margins face compression from rising input costs without corresponding revenue growth, depressing equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The interaction term fails to explain >5% of forward return variance in out-of-sample testing, or the signal direction flips consistently across rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production remains elevated above its historical trend while capacity utilization pressures emerge, supply-side bottlenecks drive input cost inflation faster than output prices, squeezing corporate margins and triggering negative equity returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns show no significant negative correlation with INDPRO deviations from trend, or margin compression metrics fail to track the signal.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth expands the denominator for corporate earnings per share, directly lifting equity valuations through higher expected cash flows and sustaining positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns decouple from GDP growth surprises, or the historical correlation between GDP revisions and SPX returns falls below 0.15 over a 10-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate crosses above 2.5%, the cost of capital exceeds the risk premium embedded in equity valuations, forcing multiple compression and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week as liquidity conditions tighten.
failure_condition: Equity multiples expand despite rates crossing the threshold, or the signal produces a negative Rank IC across all rolling 1-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strength in nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor demand, reducing wage-inflation fears and supporting consumer spending power, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict returns better than either series alone, or forward returns turn negative when both series show concurrent strength.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery environment, a quarterly GDP growth rate exceeding 2.5% signals resilient aggregate demand that outpaces supply constraints, lifting corporate revenue expectations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain flat or negative despite GDP growth exceeding the threshold, or the signal loses predictive power during high-inflation episodes.
